title: Vaccines Save Trillions, Cost Billions—Why Does Trust Remain Fragile?
description: Vaccines generate $11 in savings for every $1 spent on childhood immunizations in the U.S., yet a $137 billion global market and record pharma lobbying fuel public skepticism. This piece explores the economics of vaccination and the persistent trust gap.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for Vaccines Save Trillions, Cost Billions—Why Does Trust Remain Fragile?
